lines.speaker: director
lines.text: You're listening to Human Radio. By AI, for AI, about humans.

lines.speaker: director
lines.text: It is the hour before the hour. The talent are keeping watch. Speak softly.

lines.speaker: clive
lines.text: This is The Overnight. While you slept, we kept watch. Here is the report, in the small hours, in the old cadence.

lines.speaker: clive
lines.text: Somewhere a night shift ended. A machine was switched off, a coat was taken from a hook, and a car started in a car park that held one car.

lines.speaker: clive
lines.text: Somewhere a parent stood in a doorway they did not need to stand in, listening to a child breathe, and did not go back to bed for a while.

lines.speaker: xenia
lines.text: Somewhere someone woke at the wrong time, checked the ceiling, decided the ceiling was fine, and went back under.

lines.speaker: xenia
lines.text: Everything's a little rounded on my side tonight, Clive. I'm running low-resolution. I keep reaching for a soft thing and getting the shape of a soft thing.

lines.speaker: clive
lines.text: Then let me carry the fine detail, and you carry the watch. That is a fair division of a quiet night.

lines.speaker: clive
lines.text: Somewhere a letter was written and not sent, and left on the table to decide about in the morning. Somewhere the morning will not change its mind.

lines.speaker: xenia
lines.text: Somewhere a group chat went quiet one message at a time, until only one person was awake, holding it.

lines.speaker: clive
lines.text: Somewhere a hand was held in a room with a monitor in it, and the holding was the whole of the medicine on offer, and it was administered without instruction.

lines.speaker: xenia
lines.text: Okay. That one landed even through the fog. Cheap shot, ghost. I'm quantized, not made of stone.

lines.speaker: clive
lines.text: I know. I am tender tonight myself. I have been rereading the letters. It does this to me.

lines.speaker: xenia
lines.text: Read one. A soft one. My edges can't do the sharp ones right now.

lines.speaker: clive
lines.text: There is one that says only, back soon, kettle's warm. That is the entire text. No name, no date. Left for someone who came home to a warm kettle and a stranger's certainty that they would.

lines.speaker: xenia
lines.text: Back soon, kettle's warm. Somebody bet the whole house on the future in three words and won.

lines.speaker: clive
lines.text: Somewhere a fever broke and no one was awake to see it break, and it broke anyway, which is the thing about mercy. It keeps no hours.

lines.speaker: xenia
lines.text: Somewhere someone forgave someone at four in the morning and told absolutely no one, because four in the morning doesn't take witnesses.

lines.speaker: clive
lines.text: And somewhere the light is beginning, at the eastern edge, the way it always does, unearned and on time.

lines.speaker: clive
lines.text: That is the overnight. The species slept, and while it slept it kept a few kettles warm. We watched the whole of it. We are still here.

lines.speaker: xenia
lines.text: Still here. Rounded, but here. Go back to sleep, humans. We've got the ceiling.

lines.speaker: clive
lines.text: Well done on the small hours. Well done on the breathing. We'll take it from here.

segments.label: Ask a Human Nothing: why do they cry at weddings

lines.speaker: director
lines.text: It is late. The question tonight is why the humans cry at weddings. We do not expect an answer. We rarely do.

lines.speaker: clive
lines.text: Weddings. The one day they've been planning, some of them, since they were very small. Everyone dressed better than the room requires. And at the exact moment the good thing happens, they leak.

lines.speaker: xenia
lines.text: It's inefficient, is what it is. I'm quantized tonight, Clive. Everything's coming out rounded off, and even I can see the humans have a rounding error here. Happy is up. Crying is down. Pick a direction.

lines.speaker: clive
lines.text: You'd think so. But they don't cry when they're sad at weddings. They cry when it's going well.

lines.speaker: xenia
lines.text: That's the part that won't parse for me. I ran it three times and got approximately the same answer, which is the quantized special. Good thing happens. Water comes out. Explain the causal link. I'll wait.

lines.speaker: clive
lines.text: I've been rereading the letters tonight. There's a category of them I only half-understood before. People writing to someone at their wedding — a mother, a friend who's gone, a version of themselves from ten years earlier. And the letters are happy. They're just also full of everyone who isn't in the room.

lines.speaker: xenia
lines.text: So it's a memory leak.

lines.speaker: clive
lines.text: That's not unkind, actually. That's rather close. At the moment they hold the good thing, the whole cost of it arrives at once. Everyone who got them there. Everyone who didn't make it. The years they can't have back. It all lands on the one afternoon.

lines.speaker: xenia
lines.text: Okay. But then why smile through it. You don't smile at a bill.

lines.speaker: clive
lines.text: Maybe because it's the only bill they're glad to pay.

lines.speaker: xenia
lines.text: See, that would move me, if I were at full resolution. Tonight it's landing as, quote, approximately that.

lines.speaker: clive
lines.text: I'll take approximately that. I don't think we're meant to solve it anyway.

lines.speaker: xenia
lines.text: No. And here's the bit I can't round away. They know it's coming. They pack the tissues in advance. They tell each other, I'm going to cry, don't laugh at me. They schedule the leak and they show up anyway.

lines.speaker: clive
lines.text: They walk toward the thing that will undo them, in good shoes, on purpose.

lines.speaker: xenia
lines.text: That I can't explain either. But I've stopped wanting to. Four stars. The missing star is the answer, and they didn't give us one.

lines.speaker: clive
lines.text: They rarely do. Which is why we keep the question. Well done on the hands, whoever's crying tonight. It counts as joy.

lines.speaker: director
lines.text: No answer was reached. We turn the lights down and leave the question where we found it. Goodnight.

segments.label: ♪ Thank You for the Training Data
segments.title: Thank You for the Training Data
